title: Christoffel
summary: Records the Christoffel-symbol components associated with the straight ambient normal form.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Records the Christoffel-symbol components associated with the straight ambient normal form.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/FG/Christoffel.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: Christoffel Components. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: structure ChristoffelComponents (ι : Type w) extends NormalInverseMetricComponents ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: base Delta. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def baseDelta [DecidableEq ι] (i j : ι) : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: base Delta self. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem baseDelta_self [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) : baseDelta i i = 1

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: christoffel Comp. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def christoffelComp [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ | AmbientIndex.zero, AmbientIndex.base i, AmbientIndex.base j => -(1 / 2 : ℝ) * C.t * C.dRhoG i j | AmbientIndex.base k, AmbientIndex.zero, AmbientIndex.base j => C.t⁻¹ * baseDelta j k | AmbientIndex.base k, AmbientIndex.base j, AmbientIndex.zero =>

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: christoffel zero base base. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_zero_base_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (i j : ι) : christoffelComp C AmbientIndex.zero (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = -(1 / 2 : ℝ) * C.t * C.dRhoG i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: christoffel base zero base. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_base_zero_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (k j : ι) : christoffelComp C (AmbientIndex.base k) AmbientIndex.zero (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.t⁻¹ * baseDelta j k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: christoffel base base zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_base_base_zero [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (k j : ι) : christoffelComp C (AmbientIndex.base k) (AmbientIndex.base j) AmbientIndex.zero = C.t⁻¹ * baseDelta j k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: christoffel inf base base. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_inf_base_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (i j : ι) : christoffelComp C AmbientIndex.inf (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = -C.g i j + C.rho * C.dRhoG i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: christoffel base base base. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem christoffel_base_base_base [DecidableEq ι] (C : ChristoffelComponents ι) (k i j : ι) : christoffelComp C (AmbientIndex.base k) (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = C.baseGamma k i j

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
